title: Add batch get_activity_clusters_by_ids to eliminate N+1 in merge route
analysis: The N+1 problem: `api_merge_clusters` (review.py:406-413) loops through `body.cluster_ids`, calling `db.get_activity_cluster(cid)` per iteration — one SELECT per cluster. The fix is a batch `SELECT * FROM activity_clusters WHERE cluster_id IN (?,?...)` method returning a dict keyed by cluster_id, mirroring the existing `batch_delete_activity_clusters` (db.py:712-725) which uses the same placeholder pattern. The new method `get_activity_clusters_by_ids` goes at db.py ~line 711 (after `get_activity_clusters`, before `batch_delete_activity_clusters`). The route refactor replaces lines 404-413 with a single call plus a dict-diff check for 404s. Existing merge tests (TestApiMergeClusters) serve as regression coverage — behavior is identical, only query count changes.

steps.description: Write TestGetActivityClustersByIds.test_returns_dict_keyed_by_cluster_id — seed 3 clusters, fetch 2 by ID, assert dict has exactly 2 keys with correct data
Write TestGetActivityClustersByIds.test_returns_empty_dict_for_empty_input — call with [] and assert result is {}
Write TestGetActivityClustersByIds.test_omits_nonexistent_ids — seed c-1, fetch [c-1, nonexistent], assert dict has only c-1 key

Implement get_activity_clusters_by_ids in db.py after get_activity_clusters (~line 711). Mirror batch_delete pattern: early return {} for empty input, build placeholders, SELECT * WHERE cluster_id IN (...), return {row['cluster_id']: dict(row)} dict comprehension

Write TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch.test_merge_uses_batch_fetch — monkeypatch db.get_activity_cluster to raise AssertionError('N+1 detected'), verify merge still succeeds (proving it uses the batch path, not the per-cluster path). Seeds 2 clusters via _seed_cluster_via_db, calls POST /api/clusters/merge

Refactor api_merge_clusters in review.py: replace lines 404-413 (per-cluster for loop) with a single call to db.get_activity_clusters_by_ids(body.cluster_ids), then check for missing IDs via set difference and raise 404 for the first missing ID. Convert dict values to list for the existing `clusters` variable
Run pyright on autopilot/web/routes/review.py and confirm it reports 'possibly unbound' for `merged_map` at line 458. This is the failing static-analysis check that step-8 will fix.

Fix possibly-unbound `merged_map` in review.py: move the `merged = merged_map.get(largest_id)` lookup and the 500-check (lines 458-460) inside the try body, before the `finally` clause. The return statement stays after finally. This keeps variable scope tight and mirrors the original code where the post-merge fetch was inside try.

design_decisions.decision: Return dict[str, dict[str, object]] keyed by cluster_id, not a list
design_decisions.rationale: O(1) lookup for missing-ID detection in the merge route. The caller can detect missing IDs via set(requested_ids) - set(result.keys()) without scanning a list. This is specified in the task description.

design_decisions.decision: Place the new method between get_activity_clusters and batch_delete_activity_clusters in db.py
design_decisions.rationale: Groups all cluster-read methods together, and positions the batch-read next to the batch-delete it mirrors, maintaining logical ordering.

design_decisions.decision: Silently omit non-existent IDs from the result dict rather than raising
design_decisions.rationale: Mirrors batch_delete_activity_clusters which silently skips non-existent IDs. The caller (merge route) handles missing IDs by comparing requested vs returned keys.

design_decisions.decision: Monkeypatch test to prove batch path is used
design_decisions.rationale: Existing TestApiMergeClusters tests cover merge behavior end-to-end. A monkeypatch test that blocks the old per-cluster get_activity_cluster path proves the route was actually refactored to use the batch method, catching regression if someone reverts.

design_decisions.decision: Keep 404 detail reporting the first missing cluster_id specifically
design_decisions.rationale: Preserves the existing API contract where the 404 detail says which cluster_id was not found. Use set difference to find missing IDs, then report the first one.

design_decisions.decision: Move post-finally code inside try body rather than pre-initializing merged_map
design_decisions.rationale: Reviewer suggested either pre-initializing `merged_map = {}` before try, or moving the consumption inside try. Moving inside try is cleaner: it keeps variable scope tight, avoids a misleading empty-dict default that would never be intentionally used, and mirrors the original code where the post-merge fetch lived inside the try block.

reuse.how: Mirror the empty-check, placeholder generation, and parameterized IN clause pattern for the new SELECT query
Reuse existing db fixture and _seed_cluster helper for DB-level tests of the new method
Reuse for the monkeypatch integration test that proves the merge route uses batch fetch
Serve as regression suite — all existing merge tests must still pass after the refactor
